title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} does not support "private" context
body:
Ghost's `{{#is}}` helper supports a `private` context that matches when a publication is password-protected. Our implementation in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` only maps `home`, `index`, `post`, `page`, `tag`, `author`, `paged`. Themes that have a `private.hbs` template or guard with `{{#is "private"}}` will silently render the wrong branch.

Verify: grep Casper / Source for `{{#is "private"}}`. Build a theme that uses it and confirm Nectar never matches.

Fix: add `private` to the alias map in `registerBlockHelpers` (it always returns false unless we add a `private` flag to the route/config) and document the limitation.

title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} does not support "error" context
body:
Ghost's `{{#is}}` helper accepts `error` to detect that the current route is an error page (`error.hbs`, `error-404.hbs`). Our `RouteKind` type even includes `error` but the alias table in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` (`aliases` object) has no entry for it, so `{{#is "error"}}` will always fall through to the inverse branch.

Fix: add `error: ['error']` to the aliases map, and make sure error routes set `route.kind = 'error'`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} does not support "members" or "subscriber" context
body:
Ghost supports membership-aware contexts. While `subscriber` was removed in newer versions, themes still ship using both `{{#is "members"}}` and the inverse pattern `{{^is "subscriber"}}`. Our implementation returns false for these unknown contexts.

Fix: even if we don't have members, recognise these names and resolve them deterministically (always false because no member context exists at build time) and document the behaviour.

title: ghost-compat: {{#is}} accepts only a comma-separated string, not multiple positional args
body:
Ghost accepts both forms: `{{#is "post, page"}}` and `{{#is "post" "page"}}`. Our impl in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` flat-maps args and then splits on commas — multiple bare positional strings work, but the doc-blessed multi-arg form was never tested. Worse, if a theme passes a hash like `{{#is context="post"}}` we ignore it (Ghost doesn't support that either, so this part is OK), but the lack of tests means regressions are easy.

Fix: add tests; ensure positional + comma-split both behave.

title: ghost-compat: {{#has}} missing `count`, `any`, `all`, `count:>N`, `count:<N`, nth pattern
body:
Ghost's `{{#has}}` supports a wide surface: `tag="count:>1"`, `tag="count:1"`, `any="twitter, facebook"`, `all="@labs.x,@labs.y"`, `number="nth:3"`, `index="0"`. Our `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` implementation only handles `tag`, `author`, `visibility`, `slug`, `number` (exact equality) and a catch-all string equality. Themes commonly use `{{#has tag="count:>1"}}` to display multiple-tag UIs, and Source uses the `index`/`number` variants inside foreach.

Fix: parse `count:` prefix, comma list as OR within an attribute, `any=` / `all=` aggregators, `nth:N` modulus and ranges.

title: ghost-compat: {{#has}} `tag="a,b"` is OR within the attr, but multiple attrs should also OR (we currently OR but stop on first match for all attrs)
body:
Per Ghost docs, multiple hash attrs on `{{#has}}` are OR'd together (`{{#has tag="x" author="y"}}` → true if either). Our loop does break on first match, which is OR-correct, but we never test nested has (AND-via-nesting) and we never short-circuit `^has`. Need explicit test coverage and document that the negated form (`{{^has}}`) inverts the OR.

Fix: add tests for OR-of-attrs, nested-AND, and `{{^has}}` semantics.

title: ghost-compat: {{#has}} ignores number range patterns (e.g. number="3,6,9" inside foreach)
body:
Ghost lets you do `{{#has number="3, 6, 9"}}` inside a `{{#foreach}}` to apply a special class on those rows. Our implementation in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` does a single `Number(value)` equality check against `route.data.pagination.page`, completely missing the foreach loop variable case. This means Source's home `{{#match @number 2}}` chains that rely on similar logic do work (because they use match), but any theme that uses `{{#has number="..."}}` will break.

Fix: distinguish between the route-pagination case and the foreach-context case by reading `@number`/`@index` from `options.data`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} resource `tiers` not supported
body:
Ghost's `{{#get}}` accepts five resources: `posts`, `pages`, `tags`, `authors`, `tiers`. Our `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` handles only the first four. Themes that use `{{#get "tiers"}}` to render pricing tables will get an empty result with no warning.

Fix: at minimum, return an empty iterable for `tiers` and log a warning that membership tiers aren't supported in Nectar.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} resource `newsletters` not supported
body: Ghost added `newsletters` as a queryable resource. We don't model newsletters at all — themes querying `{{#get "newsletters"}}` will get an empty array with no signal. Same fix as tiers.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} ignores `page` (pagination) attribute
body:
Per Ghost docs, `{{#get "posts" limit="5" page="4"}}` returns items 16-20. Our implementation in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` reads `limit` and `order` but never reads `hash.page`. Themes that page through results (e.g. an "older posts" widget) will always re-render the first page.

Fix: read `hash.page`, slice accordingly, and expose the resulting pagination object to the block-param form `as |posts pagination|`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} block-param does not expose `pagination` as the second block param
body:
Ghost docs explicitly show `{{#get "posts" as |articles pages|}} {{pages.total}}`. Our `getHelper` does pass two block params (`[results, { resource }]`) but the second slot is a stub object with only `resource`, not a real pagination object with `page`, `pages`, `total`, `limit`, `prev`, `next`.

Fix: build a `PaginationInfo` for `{{#get}}` results based on `hash.limit`, `hash.page`, total items.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} ignores `include="authors,tags,count.posts"`
body:
Ghost's `include` attribute expands relationships and aggregate counts. We blindly accept whatever `include=` value but never branch on it — `count.posts` for tags/authors is already on the resolved Tag/Author objects, but the doc-blessed expectation is that without `include` those fields are not populated. Conversely, our objects always include them. Themes that test `{{#if count.posts}}` will see truthy where Ghost would see undefined. In practice this is fine, but record the divergence and add tests.

Fix: document this lossy compatibility — Nectar always populates everything; the `include` attribute is a no-op.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL supports only `+` (AND), not `,` (OR)
body:
Ghost's NQL filter supports `,` for OR (e.g. `tag:[news,intro]+featured:true` mixes both). Our `evaluateFilterExpr` in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` splits ONLY on `+`. Top-level `,` outside brackets is treated as part of a value, so `tag:news,tag:intro` is parsed as a single key/value rather than two ORed clauses.

Fix: tokenise the filter respecting bracket nesting, then handle `+` (AND) and `,` (OR).

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL does not support comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=)
body:
Ghost NQL allows e.g. `filter="published_at:>2024-01-01"` and `filter="count.posts:>5"`. Our `evaluateClause` in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` only matches exact equality and the negation prefix `-`. The `>`/`<`/`>=`/`<=` operators are not parsed.

Fix: detect the operator after the colon and dispatch to numeric/date comparison.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL does not support `null` checks (`featured:null`, `featured:-null`)
body:
Ghost's `{{recommendations filter="favicon:-null"}}` and post filters with `:null` are common. Our impl treats `null` as a literal string, so `featured:null` would match a post whose featured field is literally the string "null".

Fix: special-case `null` (and `true`/`false`) literals in `fieldMatches`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL fields restricted to a hard-coded switch
body:
`fieldMatches` in `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts` hard-codes `id`, `slug`, `featured`, `tag`, `tags`, `author`, `authors`, `visibility`, and otherwise does generic string equality. Important fields are missing: `primary_tag`, `primary_author`, `published_at`, `updated_at`, `status`, `type`, `page`, `feature_image`, `tier`. Themes filtering by `primary_tag:news` will silently miss matches because `item['primary_tag']` is an object, not a slug.

Fix: per-field accessors that drill into related objects (e.g. `primary_tag` → `item.primary_tag.slug`).

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} filter DSL interpolation `{{post.id}}` swallows {{}} braces inside filter string
body:
Source's post.hbs uses `filter="id:-{{post.id}}"` which Handlebars itself evaluates BEFORE the helper sees it. Casper uses `filter="id:-{{id}}"`. Our impl ALSO re-interpolates `{{...}}` inside the filter string against `this`, which is a duplicate of Handlebars's own substitution. If Handlebars already substituted, the second pass does nothing. But if `hash.filter` was passed as a sub-expression, the second pass may corrupt the value.

Fix: remove the manual `{{...}}` re-interpolation in `evaluateClause`. Trust Handlebars to interpolate before the helper runs.

title: ghost-compat: {{#get}} default `limit` is 15, Ghost's default is also 15 — verify behaviour when posts_per_page is requested
body:
Source uses `{{#get "posts" include="authors" limit=@config.posts_per_page}}`. We pull from `engine.config.build.posts_per_page` via `@config`, but we also expose `@config` only as the raw NectarConfig (which has `build.posts_per_page`, not top-level `posts_per_page`). So `@config.posts_per_page` is undefined here, and `parseNum(undefined) ?? 15` yields 15 — silently wrong limit.

Fix: expose a flat `@config.posts_per_page` (Ghost-style) in `buildRootData`.

title: ghost-compat: @config context is exposed as raw NectarConfig — themes expect Ghost's flat shape
body:
In `src/render/engine.ts:131`, `buildRootData` sets `config: engine.config`. Ghost themes access `@config.posts_per_page`, not `@config.build.posts_per_page`. Source's `{{#get "posts" limit=@config.posts_per_page}}` will be `undefined`, leading to the helper falling back to 15 silently.

Fix: build a Ghost-shaped `@config` object: `{ posts_per_page, image_sizes, card_assets }` from the theme package, not the Nectar config.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `members_enabled`, `paid_members_enabled`, `members_invite_only`
body:
Source's navigation, footer, header, cta, post-list partials all branch on `@site.members_enabled`, `@site.paid_members_enabled`, `@site.members_invite_only`. Our `SiteData` in `src/content/model.ts` and `buildSite` in `src/content/loader.ts` never set these. They evaluate as `undefined`/falsy, which is fine for sites that don't use members, but if a theme is built assuming "members_enabled=true" by default it will silently disable subscribe UI.

Fix: add an optional `[site.members]` config block and surface `members_enabled`, `paid_members_enabled`, `members_invite_only`, `comments_enabled` defaulting to false.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `recommendations_enabled`
body:
Source's post-list partial guards the recommendations sidebar with `{{#if @site.recommendations_enabled}}`. We never set this field on `SiteData`. As a result the recommendations block is always hidden — even if a user wants to render a static list.

Fix: add `recommendations_enabled` to `SiteData`, default false, and let users opt in via config.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `comments_access`, `comments_enabled`
body:
Casper post.hbs guards subscribe CTA with `{{#unless @site.comments_enabled}}`. Themes also branch on `@site.comments_access` to decide what footer/comments UI to show. Our `SiteData` lacks both.

Fix: add to schema and config; default `comments_enabled = false`.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing portal fields (portal_button, portal_button_icon, portal_button_signup_text, portal_button_style, portal_name, portal_plans, portal_signup_checkbox_required, portal_signup_terms_html, signup_url)
body:
Ghost themes that render their own portal triggers read these `@site.*` fields to honour admin settings. We don't expose any of them. Themes coded to gate on `@site.portal_button` will silently always hide the subscribe button.

Fix: add a `[site.portal]` config sub-table that surfaces all `portal_*` keys.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `members_support_address` and `allow_self_signup`
body:
Themes that show "Contact support: {{@site.members_support_address}}" or branch on `@site.allow_self_signup` will get undefined.

Fix: surface both via config; document defaults.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing meta fields (meta_title, meta_description, og_image, og_title, og_description, twitter_image, twitter_title, twitter_description)
body:
Ghost exposes these site-level SEO overrides via `@site.*`. Our `SiteData` doesn't model them, so themes generating fallback OG meta from `@site.og_image` etc. always get blank.

Fix: add to `SiteData`, accept in `[site]` config block, and have `{{ghost_head}}` consult them.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `admin_url`
body:
Some themes show a small admin link; some assets resolve through `@site.admin_url`. Not exposed in `SiteData`.

Fix: surface as optional config, default to `${site.url}/ghost/`.

title: ghost-compat: @site missing `codeinjection_head` and `codeinjection_foot`
body:
Ghost lets admins set site-wide head/foot code injection via Admin → Code injection. Themes read `@site.codeinjection_head`/`@site.codeinjection_foot` via `{{ghost_head}}`/`{{ghost_foot}}`. Our `SiteData` has no such fields and our `ghost_head` helper in `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts` only emits the post-level `codeinjection_head`.

Fix: add to config schema and inject in `{{ghost_head}}` / `{{ghost_foot}}`.

title: ghost-compat: @site.navigation items are plain {label,url}, missing `slug` and `current`
body:
Per Ghost docs, navigation items inside `partials/navigation.hbs` foreach expose `{{label}}`, `{{url}}`, `{{slug}}`, `{{current}}`. Our `NavigationItem` type only has `label` and `url`. Themes using `{{#if current}}` on nav items won't ever see the active state, and `{{slug}}` for nav-* CSS classes is missing.

Fix: enrich navigation items at render time with a per-route computed `slug` (slugified label) and `current` (compare against `route.url`), and expose them in the `{{navigation}}` helper's iteration context.

title: ghost-compat: {{navigation}} helper does NOT delegate to `partials/navigation.hbs` when present
body:
Ghost docs: "Create `./partials/navigation.hbs` to customize markup." If a theme defines one, Ghost uses it; otherwise default `
    …
`. Our `src/render/helpers/navigation.ts` always emits hard-coded HTML — even when `partials/navigation.hbs` exists. This means Source's navigation customisation (the `is-secondary` styling, etc.) is broken even though the partial file is loaded. Wait — Source actually invokes the partial directly (`{{> "components/navigation"}}`) so it doesn't hit this. BUT casper / many themes rely on the helper delegating. Either way we should match Ghost. Fix: in `navigationHelper`, if `engine.theme.partials['navigation']` exists, render that partial with the navigation items as context.

title: ghost-compat: {{navigation}} helper does not pass `isSecondary` to partial
body:
Ghost docs explicitly say: inside `partials/navigation.hbs` you may branch on `{{#if isSecondary}}` to detect which call (primary vs `type="secondary"`) is rendering. Our helper has no such flag because it doesn't delegate to a partial at all.

Fix: once delegation is implemented, pass `isSecondary: type === 'secondary'` to the partial's context.

title: ghost-compat: {{pagination}} helper does NOT delegate to `partials/pagination.hbs`
body:
Ghost docs: theme can override pagination by creating `partials/pagination.hbs`. Our impl in `src/render/helpers/navigation.ts` always emits hard-coded HTML. Source / Casper expect their own pagination partial to be honoured.

Fix: in `paginationHelper`, if `engine.theme.partials['pagination']` exists, render it with the pagination data as context.

title: ghost-compat: {{pagination}} helper missing the `{{page_url}}` helper for use inside pagination partial
body:
Ghost's pagination partial uses `{{page_url next}}`, `{{page_url prev}}`, `{{page_url N}}` to build page links. We never register a `page_url` helper. A custom `partials/pagination.hbs` that uses it will silently produce empty hrefs.

Fix: register a `page_url` helper that takes a number / 'prev' / 'next' and emits the corresponding URL based on `route.data.pagination.baseUrl`.

title: ghost-compat: pagination object missing `prev`/`next` integer fields under the conventional names
body:
We expose `prev` and `next` as integers (good), but Ghost also documents `prev_url` and `next_url` (we have these). However, themes also frequently iterate using `{{#if prev}}…{{/if}}` (just the integer). Currently if there's no prev, our value is `undefined` — handlebars treats that as falsy, OK. Verify with tests.

Fix: add tests that exercise both numeric and url forms.

title: ghost-compat: pagination.pages missing when posts.length === 0 (we coerce to 1)
body:
`planRoutes` in `src/build/routes.ts:118` does `if (pages.length === 0) pages.push([]);` to ensure at least one page. That means an empty site still emits `/`. But the resulting `PaginationInfo.pages = 1`, total = 0. Themes often guard `{{#if pagination.pages}}` which would render pagination on an empty site.

Fix: when there are zero posts, set `pages = 0` and skip pagination output. Or, mirror Ghost which doesn't emit pagination when there are 0 posts.

title: ghost-compat: {{img_url}} does not output Ghost's image-resize URLs (/content/images/size/wXXX/...)
body:
Ghost serves resized images at `/content/images/size/w600/2024/01/foo.jpg`. Our `img_url` in `src/render/helpers/assets.ts` ignores `size` entirely (writes the same URL irrespective), so a theme producing a `srcset="… size=\"xs\" … size=\"xxl\""` set will emit identical URLs at every width — `srcset` is useless and bandwidth multiplies.

Fix: at minimum, rewrite the URL to inject `/size/wXXX/` based on `theme.pkg.image_sizes`. Optional component to actually resize via sharp.

title: ghost-compat: {{img_url}} does not honour `format="webp"`
body:
Ghost's image API supports `format=webp|avif|jpg|png|gif` (rewrites the URL to e.g. `/content/images/size/w600/format/webp/...`). Source theme actively uses `{{img_url feature_image size="xl" format="webp"}}` in post-card.hbs. We never read `options.hash.format`.

Fix: read `hash.format`, append `/format/<value>/` to the URL.

title: ghost-compat: {{img_url}} does not look up image_sizes from the theme — it just checks key existence
body:
`src/render/helpers/assets.ts:33` does `if (size && engine.theme.pkg.image_sizes[size])` but the body of that branch (`cleaned = candidate.replace(/^\//, ''); url = '/' + cleaned`) doesn't actually USE the size to alter the URL. It only normalises a leading slash. The size attribute is effectively a no-op.

Fix: use the width from `image_sizes[size].width` to construct the Ghost-style `/content/images/size/w{width}/...` path.

title: ghost-compat: {{img_url}} does not strip Ghost CDN host before re-injecting size segment
body:
Imported Ghost content keeps absolute `https://CDN/content/images/2024/01/foo.jpg` paths. If a theme calls `{{img_url feature_image size="m"}}` on these, Ghost would rewrite the path to `/content/images/size/w600/2024/01/foo.jpg`. We bail at `if (… !candidate.startsWith('http'))`, returning the absolute URL unchanged. So srcsets containing CDN-hosted images won't be resized.

Fix: parse the URL, identify `/content/images/...` segment, and inject `size/wXXX` regardless of scheme.

title: ghost-compat: {{img_url}} returns plain string, not SafeString — theme HTML may be double-escaped via `&`
body:
Most uses are inside attribute values (`src="{{img_url …}}"`), which Handlebars will HTML-escape. That's fine. But if a theme uses `{{{img_url …}}}` (triple-stash) the value is unescaped — also fine. The risk is that the helper returns a plain string with `&` already in it (we don't escape, so OK). Trace and confirm.

Fix: write a test that asserts the literal string returned is a clean URL.

title: ghost-compat: {{asset}} helper double-escapes the result via escapeHtml even though it's used as an href
body:
`src/render/helpers/assets.ts:21` does `return new engine.hb.SafeString(escapeHtml(url + …))`. Wrapping in SafeString tells Handlebars not to escape — but we've already escaped manually, so an URL containing `&` becomes `&` and stays that way even in href/src context where Ghost emits raw `&`. The Ghost helper emits a SafeString of the raw URL.

Fix: drop the manual `escapeHtml` call on the URL; SafeString is sufficient.

title: ghost-compat: {{asset}} `hasMinFile` option is parsed but unused
body:
Ghost's `{{asset}}` accepts `hasMinFile="true"` so themes can ship both `screen.css` and `screen.min.css` and have the helper choose the latter in production. Our handler in `src/render/helpers/assets.ts:21` reads `options.hash.hasMinFile` but the ternary appends `''` either way — it's a dead read.

Fix: when truthy, look up the `.min.<ext>` variant first via the asset map.

title: ghost-compat: {{asset}} does not output the file's fingerprinted name when the file isn't .css/.js (e.g. images)
body:
`src/theme/assets.ts:33` only fingerprints `.css`, `.js`, `.mjs`. Ghost fingerprints (via `v=hash` query param) every asset reference. Theme images (logos, icons) won't bust cache. Themes may reference e.g. `{{asset "images/icon.svg"}}` — works but no cache busting.

Fix: add `?v=<hash>` query string to non-fingerprintable assets so caches still bust.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit a `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml">` for the site feed
body:
Ghost's ghost_head emits `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" title="…" href="…/rss/">`. Without it, RSS auto-discovery breaks (Reeder, browsers, podcatchers). Our `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:14` doesn't emit anything for RSS.

Fix: add the alternate link tag.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `<meta name="twitter:site">` from @site.twitter or @site.x
body:
Ghost emits both `twitter:site` (publication handle) and `twitter:creator` (per-post author handle). Our impl only emits `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`. Missing handles reduce Twitter Card validation.

Fix: include twitter:site (from `@site.twitter`) and twitter:creator (from `primary_author.twitter`).

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `<meta property="article:published_time">`, `article:modified_time`, `article:tag`, `article:author`
body:
These are OG article extensions Ghost emits on post pages. Useful for Facebook, LinkedIn, news aggregators. Currently absent in `src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts`.

Fix: when `ogType === 'article'`, emit article:* tags from post fields.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} JSON-LD missing `mainEntityOfPage`, `keywords` (tags), `wordCount`, `commentCount`
body:
Ghost's structured-data JSON-LD includes additional Schema.org fields. Our `buildJsonLd` is minimal. For SEO parity, themes that rely on rich snippets won't match Ghost.

Fix: extend the JSON-LD with `mainEntityOfPage`, `keywords` (joined tag names), `wordCount`, and (when known) `commentCount`.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} JSON-LD only emits Article on post pages, never Person/WebSite for author/tag pages
body:
Ghost emits `@type: Person` for author pages, `@type: Series` for tag pages (or similar). Our `buildJsonLd` returns the generic `WebSite` for anything that's not a post.

Fix: branch on `route.kind` and emit Person / Series / WebPage as appropriate.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `<link rel="amphtml">` or AMP support
body:
Ghost emits an AMP link when AMP is enabled and `amp.hbs` exists. We don't generate AMP routes at all (see separate gap), and don't emit the link.

Fix: scope-out AMP; document the absence; ensure no `amphtml` reference accidentally leaks.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not emit `accent_color` CSS variable inline
body:
Ghost themes get `<style>:root { --ghost-accent-color: #xxx; }</style>` injected by `ghost_head` from the site's accent_color setting. Source's `default.hbs` works around this by manually emitting `--background-color: {{@custom.site_background_color}}` but Casper relies on Ghost's auto-injected `--ghost-accent-color`.

Fix: emit `<style>:root{--ghost-accent-color:{{@site.accent_color}}}</style>` from ghost_head.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_head}} does not inject Portal/Members/Search/Sodo-search JavaScript
body:
Ghost's real `ghost_head` injects `<script src="…/portal.min.js" data-i18n="…" data-ghost="…">` and the search SDK. Without these, `data-portal` buttons and `data-ghost-search` triggers in themes do nothing. Our docs note this is out of scope, but at minimum we should emit a comment / log so theme devs aren't confused.

Fix: opt-in `[components.portal]` and `[components.search]` that inject stub or real client scripts.

title: ghost-compat: {{ghost_foot}} does not emit the site-level codeinjection_foot, only the post-level
body:
`src/render/helpers/ghost-head.ts:54` reads `ctx.codeinjection_foot` (post/page field) but never appends the site's `@site.codeinjection_foot`. On non-post routes (home, tag, author) it emits nothing.

Fix: concatenate `site.codeinjection_foot` + `ctx.codeinjection_foot`.

title: ghost-compat: `body_class` does not include `hash-<slug>` for internal-tag slugs
body:
Ghost's body_class includes `tag-<slug>` AND, when a tag is internal (slug starts with `hash-`), often a `hash-…` class. Our `computeBodyClass` only adds `tag-<slug>`. Themes that style hidden hash-tags need both.

Fix: when on a tag route and `tag.visibility === 'internal'`, also push `hash-…`.

title: ghost-compat: `body_class` missing `post-template-<slug>`, `page-template-<slug>`, `author-template-<slug>`, `tag-template-<slug>` per-resource modifiers
body:
Ghost emits `post-template-<slug>` etc. so themes can target a specific post in CSS. Our `computeBodyClass` only emits the generic `post-template`. Themes with per-post CSS rules need the per-slug class.

Fix: append `post-template-${post.slug}` (and equivalent for page/tag/author).

title: ghost-compat: `body_class` missing `error-template` on error pages and `members-template` on members area
body:
Real Ghost emits these. Since we don't have error or members routes (see separate gaps), this is downstream.

Fix: when error routes are added, include `error-template` and `error-<statusCode>` body classes.

title: ghost-compat: `post_class` missing `no-image`, `featured`, `access` modifiers
body:
Ghost emits `no-image` when post has no feature image, and visibility-based access classes. Our `computePostClass` in `src/render/engine.ts:165` only handles `tag-<slug>` + `featured`.

Fix: append `no-image` when `!post.feature_image`, append `access` / `members-only` / `paid-only` based on visibility.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} default format is hard-coded "DD MMM YYYY", Ghost default is `ll` (locale-aware short)
body:
Per docs, when called without options Ghost uses Moment's `ll` format (e.g. "Jan 15, 2026"). Our `src/render/helpers/date.ts:31` defaults to `"DD MMM YYYY"`. Themes that omit format will produce a different visual result than on Ghost.

Fix: change default to `'ll'` and ensure dayjs/localizedFormat handles it.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} does not honour `locale=` hash override
body:
Ghost docs: `{{date format="MMMM Do, YYYY" locale="fr-fr"}}`. Our impl never reads `options.hash.locale`. Multi-locale themes can't override per-call.

Fix: read `hash.locale`, call `dayjs.locale(locale)` for that invocation.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} `timeago` accepts only `true`/'true' string, not Ghost's `timeago` (no value) idiom
body:
Ghost supports `{{date timeago="true"}}` and bare `{{date timeago=true}}`. We check `options.hash.timeago === true || options.hash.timeago === 'true'`. The `=true` (boolean from Handlebars) form is fine, but Handlebars sometimes coerces the string `'true'` for hash-without-value attributes. Verify both paths.

Fix: also accept the string `'true'` and any truthy.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} `timezone=` hash override not implemented
body:
Ghost lets themes override the global timezone with `{{date timezone="Europe/Paris"}}`. We always use `engine.content.site.timezone`. Per-helper override absent.

Fix: read `hash.timezone`, fall back to site default.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} uses dayjs but doesn't load locale data per @site.locale
body:
`src/render/helpers/date.ts` imports dayjs and `localizedFormat`, but never imports/loads non-English locale data. Sites set to `de`, `fr`, etc. will still output English month names. Themes calling `{{date format="MMMM"}}` will all say "January".

Fix: dynamically import `dayjs/locale/<locale>.js` based on `site.locale` and call `dayjs.locale(locale)`.

title: ghost-compat: {{date}} doesn't gracefully handle invalid dates — emits 'Invalid Date'
body:
Themes may pass non-date data; Ghost emits an empty string or falls back. dayjs returns 'Invalid Date' as a literal string. Ghost themes that render `{{date custom_field}}` would show garbage.

Fix: check `dayjs(value).isValid()` and return empty string when invalid.

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} translation interpolation uses `{name}` braces but Ghost docs `{name}` AND positional `%` — verify ICU MessageFormat fragments
body:
`src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:22` does `out.replaceAll('{key}', value)`. Source's footer uses `{{t "Powered by {ghostlink}" ghostlink="…"}}` — works. But Ghost supports plural-form ICU patterns (`{count, plural, one {…} other {…}}`); we don't.

Fix: at least document the limitation; long-term, integrate a real ICU lib (e.g. `@formatjs/intl-messageformat`).

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} doesn't honour theme-translations key fallback (en.json shared keys empty → fallback to literal key)
body:
Source's en.json has empty values (`" (Page %)": ""`). Our code in `src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:12` does `active[key] ?? fallback[key] ?? key` — but an empty string is NOT nullish, so the empty value passes through and `{{t " (Page %)"}}` produces `""`. Ghost falls back to the key when the value is empty.

Fix: treat empty string as fallback trigger: `active[key] || fallback[key] || key`.

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} `%` positional placeholder substitutes the FIRST hash value indiscriminately
body:
`interpolate` in `src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:30` replaces every `%` with `Object.values(hash)[0]`. Source's `{{reading_time minute=(t "1 min read") minutes=(t "% min read")}}` works because the `reading_time` helper passes `count` separately. But any template that hash-passes other named values (e.g. `numberOfPosts=12`) AND has a `%` somewhere would substitute the wrong value because hash key order is non-deterministic.

Fix: prefer a numbered positional `%1`, `%2` or document that `%` only substitutes the singular numeric value.

title: ghost-compat: {{t}} doesn't expose the `{{lang}}` short-form alias documented by Ghost
body:
Ghost has `{{lang}}` (returns locale code) — we have it (`src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:16`). However the docs also show a helper called `translate` (utility category alias for `t`). We don't register `translate`.

Fix: register `translate` as an alias of `t`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{plural}}` does not handle Ghost's `count, plural, one, other` ICU style or pluralisation by locale
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/strings.ts:18` accepts `singular=`, `plural=`, `empty=` hashes, and substitutes `%`. Ghost's `{{plural}}` is similar (legacy) but newer themes use ICU via `{{t}}`. Document the limitation; tests show our helper works for English-only.

Fix: at minimum add a `dual=` hash and language-pack-aware switch.

title: ghost-compat: {{authors}} does NOT support `separator`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `limit`, `from`, `to`, `visibility`, `autolink`
body:
Per docs, `{{authors}}` mirrors the `{{tags}}` helper. Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:46` only joins by `", "` (hard-coded) and emits raw names. Source uses `{{authors autolink="false" separator=", "}}` in post-card.hbs — those hashes are silently ignored, autolinking is OFF in our impl regardless.

Fix: bring `{{authors}}` to feature parity with `{{tags}}` (which itself has gaps below).

title: ghost-compat: {{tags}} does not support `prefix`, `suffix`, `limit`, `from`, `to`, `visibility`
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:60` reads only `separator` and `autolink`. Missing the rest. Themes that do `{{tags prefix="Tagged: " limit="3" visibility="public"}}` get no prefix and a full unfiltered list.

Fix: implement the full hash surface.

title: ghost-compat: {{tags}} does not filter internal `hash-` tags by default — Ghost hides them unless `visibility="all"`
body:
Ghost's default is `visibility="public"` which omits internal tags (slug starts with `hash-`). Our impl emits ALL tags. Themes building visible tag lists will leak internal admin tags.

Fix: default to public-only; require `visibility="all"` to include internal.

title: ghost-compat: `{{tags}}` does not output `accent_color` field on tag objects, but Ghost themes read it
body:
Tag objects in Ghost expose `accent_color`. Our `Tag` model in `src/content/model.ts:43` lacks the field. Themes that render colored tag pills break.

Fix: add `accent_color: string | undefined` to Tag, read from frontmatter.

title: ghost-compat: `Tag` model missing `og_image`, `og_title`, `og_description`, `twitter_image`, `twitter_title`, `twitter_description`, `codeinjection_head`, `codeinjection_foot`, `canonical_url`
body:
Ghost tags have per-tag SEO and OG overrides. Our model only has meta_title/meta_description. The `tag.hbs` ghost_head won't pick up these fields for tag-archive OG sharing.

Fix: add to `Tag` model and `normalizeTag` in `src/content/loader.ts`.

title: ghost-compat: `Author` model missing `og_image`, `twitter_image`, `codeinjection_*`, `accent_color`
body:
Same as tags — per-author social/SEO overrides are missing. Themes that show per-author social images on archive pages won't get them.

Fix: extend `Author` model in `src/content/model.ts` and parsing in `normalizeAuthor`.

title: ghost-compat: `Author` model missing `url` consistency — currently set to `/author/<slug>/` but Ghost uses absolute URLs in some contexts
body:
Our `normalizeAuthor` (`src/content/loader.ts:275`) builds the URL via `joinUrl(config.site.url, …)` — that's already absolute. But the `Post.url` (line 311) is also absolute. Themes calling `{{url}}` on relative output and `{{url absolute=true}}` on absolute output may double-prefix. Verify by snapshot.

Fix: store URLs as paths (relative) and let `{{url}}` decide absolute vs relative.

title: ghost-compat: `Post.url`, `Page.url`, `Tag.url`, `Author.url` all baked as absolute strings — Ghost stores them as paths
body:
In Ghost, themes call `{{url}}` (relative) or `{{url absolute="true"}}` (absolute). Our model stores absolute URLs (`joinUrl(site.url, '/post/')`). When a theme calls `{{url}}` without `absolute`, our `url` helper returns the same absolute string anyway — no relative form exists. This breaks self-hosted sites with paths like `/blog/` and breaks `<a href="{{url}}">` patterns that expect a relative.

Fix: store the path internally; let `urlHelper` apply absolute prefix.

title: ghost-compat: {{url}} helper doesn't accept a positional argument — only reads `this.url`
body:
`src/render/helpers/urls.ts:17` reads `ctx.url`. Ghost's `{{url}}` accepts a parameter: `{{url '/tag/foo/'}}` and `{{url absolute="true"}}`. Themes that pass an arbitrary string to `{{url}}` will get empty.

Fix: accept first positional arg, fall back to `this.url`.

title: ghost-compat: {{url}} helper does not accept `data` block params from `{{#get}}`
body:
Ghost's `{{url tag}}` inside a foreach-over-get accepts the iterated object directly. Our impl ignores any positional arg.

Fix: accept any positional `{ url }` object and prefer its `.url`.

title: ghost-compat: `readable_url` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's `{{readable_url}}` outputs a human-friendly URL (strips protocol, www, trailing slash). Used in bookmark cards. Not registered in `src/render/helpers/`.

Fix: register `readable_url` helper.

title: ghost-compat: `social_accounts` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's `{{#social_accounts}}` block iterates connected social handles on the site or author. Themes use it to render social icons dynamically. Not in our helper list.

Fix: register a block helper that iterates `[twitter, facebook, instagram, ...]` present on the active context.

title: ghost-compat: {{social_url}} only supports a fixed list, missing `x`, `youtube_channel`, `github`, `mailto`
body:
Source/Casper authors may set X (formerly Twitter) handles, GitHub, etc. Our `SOCIAL_PATTERNS` covers twitter/facebook/linkedin/bluesky/mastodon/threads/tiktok/youtube/instagram only. Newer themes add `x` and `github`.

Fix: extend SOCIAL_PATTERNS; treat `x` and `twitter` interchangeably.

title: ghost-compat: {{social_url}} ignores `social_url="@site"` and `social_url="author"` positional targets
body:
Ghost's `{{social_url type="twitter"}}` reads from current scope, but you can also pass `{{social_url "@site" type="twitter"}}`. We ignore positional args. Themes rendering both author and site social blocks from one helper invocation can't switch.

Fix: read first positional arg as the lookup scope.

title: ghost-compat: `twitter_url` and `facebook_url` direct helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost also has `{{twitter_url}}` / `{{facebook_url}}` as standalone helpers (deprecated but used in old themes). We have only `social_url`.

Fix: register the deprecated aliases or document the requirement to use `social_url`.

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} doesn't support `columns` and the `@rowStart`/`@rowEnd` flags
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:21` sets `data.rowStart = false; data.rowEnd = false;` unconditionally — never honouring the `columns=` hash option. Themes that do `{{#foreach posts columns=3}}` for grid layouts won't get `@rowStart`/`@rowEnd`, breaking row-aware CSS.

Fix: when `hash.columns` is set, compute `i % columns === 0` (rowStart) and `(i+1) % columns === 0 || i === last` (rowEnd).

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} `from`/`to` semantics are 1-indexed inclusive — but our `to` defaults to `min(items.length, from+limit-1)` which conflicts with Ghost's documented behaviour
body:
Ghost docs: `from` and `to` are 1-indexed inclusive bounds. Without `to`, all remaining items are emitted (limited by `limit`). Our `parseNum(options.hash.to) ?? Math.min(items.length, from + limit - 1)` interacts badly when `to` is omitted AND `limit` is omitted (limit defaults to items.length → to = items.length+from-1 which is past end; slice handles it but mismatches Ghost).

Fix: separate the three: `from = hash.from ?? 1`, `to = hash.to ?? items.length`, `limit = hash.limit ?? Infinity`. Apply slice(from-1, to) then take .slice(0, limit).

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} `as |alias|` block-param syntax not honoured
body:
Source/Casper use `{{#get "posts" as |alias|}}{{#foreach alias}}…{{/foreach}}{{/get}}` which works because Handlebars itself binds the block param. But Ghost also allows `{{#foreach posts as |post|}}` to alias the iterated item. Our `foreachHelper` calls `options.fn(item, …)` without block-param plumbing, so `as |post|` is silently ignored — the inner template sees the item as `this`, which is normally fine, but breaks templates using `{{post.title}}` rather than `{{title}}`.

Fix: when `options.fn.blockParams > 0`, pass `[item, { /* foreach state */ }]` as the second arg.

title: ghost-compat: {{#foreach}} `visibility="all|public|none|members|paid"` filter is partial
body:
Our `visibilityFilter` (`src/render/helpers/blocks.ts:211`) accepts `all` (always true) and equality match. It correctly handles `members` and `paid` because we set `obj.visibility` to those exact strings. But `visibility="none"` (display-only-hidden) is documented but not specially handled — `(obj.visibility ?? 'public') === 'none'` would never match because no item has visibility "none".

Fix: define `none` = filter out everything except items with visibility marker matching admin's `none`/`internal` visibility. Document the intent.

title: ghost-compat: `{{content}}` helper does not gate by `access` (visibility/members)
body:
Ghost's `{{content}}` automatically renders the paywall (truncates content and inserts portal CTA) when a member doesn't have access. Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:5` renders the full HTML unconditionally. For `members`/`paid` posts on a public render, we leak full content — exact opposite of Ghost.

Fix: when `visibility !== 'public'` and no member is signed in, truncate at the paywall marker and insert a paywall snippet (or skip body entirely).

title: ghost-compat: `{{content}}` `words=` option truncates HTML by stripping tags, breaking semantic markup
body:
Our `truncateWords` in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:147` regex-strips all HTML and joins by space — the result is plaintext, not HTML, so the SafeString wrapper outputs broken excerpts in places themes expect HTML.

Fix: write a real HTML-aware truncator that closes open tags (e.g. truncate-html or a hand-rolled one).

title: ghost-compat: `{{excerpt}}` `characters=` truncation has no smart-word-boundary handling
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:30` slices at exact character count. Ghost soft-stops at the last word boundary, optionally appending `…`. Source uses `{{excerpt words="20"}}` (which we implement via `truncateWordsText`, OK) but `characters=` is naive.

Fix: when `characters` is set, slice and back up to the last whitespace.

title: ghost-compat: `{{reading_time}}` helper uses fixed strings, not localised via `{{t}}`
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/content.ts:34` reads `options.hash.minute` and `options.hash.minutes` defaults `'1 min read'`. Source / Casper pass localised strings via `{{t "1 min read"}}` so it works in their flow. But callers that omit the hash get English. Ghost's default also is English, so OK — but document.

Fix: docs note.

title: ghost-compat: `{{meta_title}}` does not append `" (Page %)"` translation correctly on multi-page archives
body:
Source's default.hbs uses `{{meta_title page=(t " (Page %)")}}`. Our `metaTitleHelper` in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:79` reads `hash.page` and replaces `%` with `pagination.page`. The translated string is `" (Page %)"` (with leading space). Issue: we only emit the suffix when `pagination.page > 1`. The leading space stays, so output becomes `"Site - Page 2"` — wait actually format is `${baseTitle}${pageSuffix.replace('%', String(pagination.page))}` → `"Site (Page 2)"` — looks correct on archive pages. But on tag/author pages we don't pass a `pageSuffix`. Ghost emits `"Tag - Sitename (Page 2)"` style. Verify and align.

Fix: also append page suffix on tag/author paginated views (when `route.kind === 'tag'|'author'`).

title: ghost-compat: `{{meta_title}}` does not include site title in tag/author pages
body:
Ghost emits `{{tag.name}} - {{site.title}}` style on tag pages by default; we just emit `tag.name`. SEO/preview impact.

Fix: combine resource name with site title for non-post/page routes.

title: ghost-compat: `{{meta_description}}` falls back to `excerpt` instead of `site.description` when on tag/author routes
body:
On tag pages, our `metaDescriptionHelper` returns `ctx.meta_description ?? ctx.excerpt ?? site.description`. But on a tag context, `excerpt` doesn't exist (the Tag model has no excerpt). Same for authors. Tag pages will fall back to `site.description`, but for authors, `bio` is the analogue and we don't check it.

Fix: per-context fallback chain (tag → description, author → bio).

title: ghost-compat: `meta_data` helper not implemented (referenced in docs as a data helper)
body:
Per Ghost docs there's a data helper `{{meta_data}}` that returns the full SEO blob (not just title/description) used for structured-data output. Used less today, but Casper-derived themes may reference it.

Fix: register a stub helper that returns an object with `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `og_*`, `twitter_*`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{prev_post}}` and `{{next_post}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost's docs list `{{#prev_post}}…{{/prev_post}}` / `{{#next_post}}…{{/next_post}}` block helpers that scope to the previous/next post on `post.hbs`. Our `Post` model has `prev` and `next` fields (good) but no helpers expose them as Ghost-style blocks. Themes coded against the helper get nothing.

Fix: register `prev_post` and `next_post` as block helpers that read `this.prev` / `this.next`.

title:
ghost-compat: `{{comments}}` outputs `
` — Ghost emits a full Comments embed
body: Our stub in `src/render/helpers/content.ts:112` emits a div with `data-nectar-comments`. Ghost emits `

title: ghost-compat: `{{comment_count}}` helper not implemented
body:
Themes display "3 comments" via `{{comment_count empty="" singular="comment" plural="comments"}}`. Our helpers do not register this. Templates using it render `(empty string)`.

Fix: register `{{comment_count}}` returning 0 with proper singular/plural handling.

title: ghost-compat: `{{subscribe_form}}` stub doesn't post anywhere — but Ghost expects `<form data-members-form action="…">`
body:
Our stub emits `<form data-nectar-subscribe action="#" method="post">`. Themes targeting Ghost's `data-members-form` attribute won't get progressive enhancement.

Fix: change attribute to `data-members-form` and include the documented hidden inputs Ghost emits.

title: ghost-compat: `{{input_email}}` does not emit Ghost's required class `gh-input` and `data-members-email` correctly with the surrounding form
body:
We emit `<input data-members-email type="email" required …>`. That's mostly right but misses `class="gh-input"` and the parent form context that Portal hooks into.

Fix: add the conventional class; document that without a Portal script the input is non-functional.

title: ghost-compat: `{{input_password}}` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's private/password-protected sites use `private.hbs` with `{{input_password}}` rendering the password form. Helper absent.

Fix: register as a stub (since we don't support private sites) that emits `<input type="password" name="password">`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{cancel_link}}` helper not implemented
body: Themes that render a portal `Cancel subscription` link use `{{cancel_link}}`. Absent from our helpers. Templates render empty. Fix: register a stub helper emitting `Cancel`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{price}}` helper not implemented
body:
Ghost's `{{price}}` formats a price with currency and interval. Used in pricing UIs. Absent.

Fix: register a stub that formats `{{price tier.monthly_price}}` → `$N/mo`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{tier}}` and `{{tiers}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost themes can iterate tiers with `{{#tiers}}…{{/tiers}}` and `{{#tier}}…{{/tier}}` on the post. Absent in our helpers and in our `Post`/`Page` models.

Fix: model tiers (optional, gated on members enabled); register helpers.

title: ghost-compat: `{{products}}` and `{{product}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Older Ghost theme API used "products" before "tiers". Casper-vintage themes still reference them. Helper absent.

Fix: alias to `{{tiers}}` / `{{tier}}` for back-compat.

title: ghost-compat: `{{total_members}}` and `{{total_paid_members}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost data helpers documented: "Outputs the number of members, rounded and humanised." Absent. Themes that show "Join 10k+ subscribers" will render empty.

Fix: register helpers; default to 0 with a config-overridable static number.

title: ghost-compat: `{{recommendations}}` helper not implemented (Source uses it!)
body:
`src/render/helpers/` has NO `recommendations` registration. Source's `partials/components/post-list.hbs:114` invokes `{{recommendations}}`. Currently the unregistered helper is rendered as `(empty)` by Handlebars — themes show a blank section under an `

Recommendations

` header. Fix: register `recommendations` as a no-op or read from an optional `[recommendations]` config table; gate by `@site.recommendations_enabled`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{search}}` utility helper not implemented
body:
Ghost has a `{{search}}` utility helper that outputs a pre-styled search button + icon (uses Ghost's hosted search SDK). Absent. Source uses its own `data-ghost-search` triggers, so unaffected, but Casper-derived themes may invoke `{{search}}`.

Fix: register a stub that outputs the equivalent button markup.

title: ghost-compat: `{{log}}` debug helper not implemented
body:
Ghost has `{{log foo}}` to console.log values in development. Useful for theme dev. Absent.

Fix: register `log` to print to stdout when `process.env.NECTAR_DEBUG`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{json}}` helper not implemented
body:
Per docs (utility): "Safely serialize values for inline JSON output." Used by themes embedding analytics or hydration payloads.

Fix: register `json` that JSON.stringify-encodes its argument and wraps in SafeString.

title: ghost-compat: `{{block}}` and `{{contentFor}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Documented utility helpers: "Used along with `{{contentFor}}` to pass data up and down the template hierarchy." Themes use them to inject head-block content from child templates into `default.hbs`. Absent.

Fix: register handlebars-layouts plugin or hand-roll block/contentFor.

title: ghost-compat: `{{partials}}` registration helper not implemented
body:
Documented utility: "Include chunks of reusable template code." The `{{partials}}` helper is shorthand for registering shared markup. Themes use it as a directive, but mostly they just call `{{> name}}` directly. Document and confirm whether action is needed.

Fix: document; usually no work required.

title: ghost-compat: `{{contrast_text_color}}` and `{{color_to_rgba}}` helpers not implemented
body:
Ghost utility helpers: convert colors. Used by themes to derive a readable text color from a user-chosen background (Source's inline JS replicates this manually, but Casper uses the helper). Absent in our helpers.

Fix: register both: `color_to_rgba("#fff", alpha=0.5)` returns `rgba(…)`; `contrast_text_color("#fff")` returns `"dark"` or `"light"`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{split}}` utility helper not implemented
body:
Documented: "Split a string into one or more iterable strings." Some themes use it to parse comma-separated config values.

Fix: register `split` that takes a string + separator and returns an array iterable by foreach.

title: ghost-compat: `{{link}}` helper does not honour `data-*` attributes (e.g. data-portal)
body:
Ghost's `{{#link}}…{{/link}}` accepts arbitrary `data-*` hash attributes. Our `src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:40` only honours `href`, `class`, `target`. Themes that do `{{#link href="…" data-portal="signin"}}` will lose the `data-portal` attribute.

Fix: pass through any `data-*` hash attr verbatim.

title: ghost-compat: `{{link}}` helper does not auto-add `nav-current` / active class based on URL match
body:
Ghost's `{{#link}}` automatically adds an active class when its `href` matches the current route. Useful for navigation. Our impl does not. Themes calling `{{#link href="/about/" activeClass="current"}}` get no auto-class.

Fix: integrate the `link_class` logic into `link` so the active class is auto-injected.

title: ghost-compat: `{{link_class}}` only matches exact URL, not subpaths
body:
`src/render/helpers/navigation.ts:50` compares with `normaliseUrl` (strips trailing slash). Ghost matches on subpath: e.g. `/tag/news/` should make the `/tag/` nav item active. Our impl returns empty.

Fix: when the `for` URL is a prefix of the current URL, treat as active.

title: ghost-compat: `{{concat}}` does not accept SafeStrings (loses HTML safety)
body:
Our `concatHelper` in `src/render/helpers/strings.ts:5` calls `args.slice(0, -1).map(String)` which on a SafeString instance returns `[object Object]`. Themes that concat a partial output with a literal will get garbage.

Fix: detect SafeString and call `.toString()` properly; consider returning SafeString when any input is SafeString.

title: ghost-compat: `{{encode}}` only does `encodeURIComponent` — Ghost also encodes for HTML attribute contexts
body:
Our impl `encodeURIComponent(String(value ?? ''))` is URL encoding only. Ghost docs say "Encode text to be safely used in a URL" — matches our behaviour. OK, but verify the precise edge cases (spaces become `%20`, not `+`).

Fix: add tests asserting behaviour against Ghost's examples.

title: ghost-compat: `{{or}}` / `{{and}}` / `{{not}}` / `{{eq}}` are Nectar-only — Ghost doesn't ship these
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/flow.ts` registers `or`, `and`, `not`, `eq`. These are not Ghost helpers. Themes won't depend on them, but if a Nectar-tuned theme uses them and is later moved to Ghost, it breaks. Document as Nectar extensions.

Fix: docs note in `docs/GHOST_COMPATIBILITY.md`.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#match}}` does not support `or` chains (Ghost supports `{{else match}}`) — verify behaviour
body:
Source heavily uses `{{#match … }} … {{else match …}} … {{/match}}`. Handlebars treats `{{else match …}}` as `{{else}}{{#match …}}…{{/match}}` (a chained helper). Our `matchHelper` doesn't track the chained-else state but Handlebars handles it natively. Verify with a test.

Fix: add explicit test for the `else match` chain.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#match}}` truthy-only mode swallows the inverse return on inline (non-block) invocation
body:
When `options.fn` is undefined (inline `{{match …}}`), our `matchHelper` returns the raw `result` boolean. Ghost returns the empty string for false and the matched value for true. Themes inline'ing `{{match val "x"}}` to print a class may get the literal string "false".

Fix: when inline and `!result`, return empty string. When inline and `result` and only one arg, return the truthy operand.

title: ghost-compat: `{{#match}}` `~` substring operator does not work for arrays
body:
Ghost lets you do `{{#match @custom.header_style "~" "Landing,Search"}}` — string-contains. Our `compare` function casts both to String and uses `.includes()`. Works for strings. But Ghost ALSO permits matching against an array — our impl forces toString which yields `"Landing,Search"` and substring-matches against that — actually mostly OK. Verify edge cases (`null`, undefined, numbers).

Fix: add tests.

title: ghost-compat: routes.yaml support missing entirely
body:
Ghost ships `content/settings/routes.yaml` that defines collections, channels, taxonomies, and custom routes. Themes ship `routes.yaml` examples that customise permalinks (e.g. `/blog/{slug}/`) and add channels (e.g. `/featured/`). Our `src/build/routes.ts` hard-codes the route shape with no configuration surface beyond `posts_per_page`. Themes that rely on a custom permalink pattern won't work.

Fix: add `routes.yaml` loader + planner that supports collections, channels, taxonomies, and `template`/`data` mappings.

title: ghost-compat: redirects.yaml support missing
body:
Ghost users migrate via `content/data/redirects.yaml`. We have no redirect mechanism — imports lose their redirect history.

Fix: load `redirects.yaml` (and legacy `redirects.json`); emit a `_redirects` file or per-host static HTML redirects.

title: ghost-compat: Channels (multi-tag aggregation routes) not supported
body:
Ghost's `controller: channel` lets users define routes like `/apple-news/` with `filter: tag:[iphone,ipad,mac]`. We have no concept of channels — themes won't render them.

Fix: in `planRoutes`, after loading routes.yaml, emit channel routes with the appropriate `template` and filtered post list.

title: ghost-compat: Collections with custom `permalink` patterns not supported
body:
Ghost's collections accept `permalink: /{primary_tag}/{slug}/` to drive URL structure. Our `planRoutes` hard-codes `/{slug}/`. Themes (and existing imports) that expect `/blog/post-slug/` style routes break.

Fix: introduce a permalink template engine that supports `{slug}`, `{primary_tag}`, `{primary_author}`, `{year}`, `{month}`, `{day}`, `{id}`.

title: ghost-compat: Taxonomies are hard-coded to `/tag/` and `/author/` paths
body:
Ghost's routes.yaml lets you remap or disable taxonomies (e.g. `tag: /topics/{slug}/`). We hard-code `/tag/<slug>/` and `/author/<slug>/` in `src/build/routes.ts:65` and `:90`. Cannot customise.

Fix: derive these paths from routes.yaml (or `[taxonomies]` config block).

title: ghost-compat: Custom static routes (`/features/: features`) not supported
body:
Ghost lets you map any URL to any template. We have no mechanism to add additional static routes. Themes shipping with companion routes.yaml (e.g. a `/podcast/` overview page) need this.

Fix: support route entries `path: template` and `path: { template, data, content_type }`.

title: ghost-compat: `data: post.slug` / `data: page.slug` linking on custom routes not supported
body:
Ghost can attach a post/page object to a custom route so a static template can render it. We have no equivalent.

Fix: implement after routes.yaml; allow `data` field to inject a single resource into the route's render context.

title: ghost-compat: `content_type` override for custom routes not supported
body:
Ghost lets routes emit `text/xml` etc. for custom feeds. We emit only HTML. Themes that ship a custom JSON or XML feed in routes.yaml can't be replicated.

Fix: pass through to the emitter; set file extension and Content-Type appropriately.

title: ghost-compat: Per-collection RSS not supported (`/blog/rss/` for collection `/blog/`)
body:
Ghost auto-emits RSS at `<collection-path>/rss/`. Currently we emit only a single global RSS (in `components.rss`). Themes with multiple collections lose feeds.

Fix: once collections land, emit RSS per collection.

title: ghost-compat: RSS feed structure not implemented at all (no `src/build/feeds.ts` file present)
body:
DESIGN.md §3.6 says `feeds.ts` emits sitemap + RSS, but no such file exists in `src/build/`. The config schema has `components.rss.enabled` and `components.sitemap.enabled` but the implementation is missing. Themes shipping `<link rel=alternate type=application/rss+xml>` references will 404.

Fix: implement `src/build/feeds.ts` per design.

title: ghost-compat: When RSS is implemented, it must emit Ghost-conformant channel/items
body:
Ghost's RSS includes: channel (title, description, link, generator, language, lastBuildDate, atom:link self-reference, image), items (title, description, link, guid permaLink=false, category for each tag, dc:creator, pubDate, media:content for feature_image, content:encoded with full HTML). Many readers depend on these.

Fix: emit a Ghost-shape RSS 2.0 feed with all standard extension namespaces (dc, atom, media, content).

title: ghost-compat: RSS items must use `<![CDATA[...]]>` for HTML, not entity-escaped
body:
Ghost's RSS wraps content:encoded in CDATA so HTML renders in readers. If we naively XML-escape, readers (Feedly, NetNewsWire) show raw `

` tags. Fix: emit CDATA blocks for title (when special chars) and content:encoded.

title: ghost-compat: RSS guid should be the post URL with `isPermaLink="true"`
body:
Ghost emits `<guid isPermaLink="true">{{url absolute=true}}</guid>`. Without this, feed readers will not de-duplicate items correctly across cache windows.

Fix: include guid in standard form.

title: ghost-compat: Sitemap file structure not implemented at all
body:
Ghost emits five files: `/sitemap.xml` (index), `/sitemap-pages.xml`, `/sitemap-posts.xml`, `/sitemap-tags.xml`, `/sitemap-authors.xml`. The config schema enables sitemap (`components.sitemap.enabled = true`) but no code emits the files (`src/build/feeds.ts` is missing).

Fix: implement multi-file sitemap matching Ghost's structure.

title: ghost-compat: Sitemap items must include `<image:image>` with feature images
body:
Ghost includes Schema.org image extension in sitemaps (image:loc, image:caption). Search engines use this for image indexing.

Fix: when emitting sitemap-posts.xml, add `<image:image><image:loc>…</image:loc></image:image>` for each post with a feature_image.

title: ghost-compat: Sitemap `lastmod` should reflect updated_at, not just published_at
body:
Ghost uses the latest of updated_at / published_at. We should follow suit so search engines refresh on edits.

Fix: use Math.max(updated, published) timestamp.

title: ghost-compat: robots.txt support — Ghost emits a default unless theme provides one
body:
Ghost serves a default `/robots.txt` (disallows admin paths, advertises sitemap). Theme may override. We don't ship a default; if a theme ships `robots.txt` we don't propagate it.

Fix: copy `robots.txt` from theme root if present; emit a default sitemap-referencing one otherwise.

title: ghost-compat: AMP route (`amp.hbs` + `/<slug>/amp/` URL) not generated
body:
Ghost optionally renders AMP versions of posts via `amp.hbs`. Our route planner doesn't emit any AMP routes. Themes that ship `amp.hbs` (Casper does) won't have it rendered.

Fix: when `amp.hbs` exists, emit `/<slug>/amp/index.html` for each post; emit corresponding `<link rel=amphtml>` in canonical post head.

title: ghost-compat: error.hbs / error-404.hbs templates never rendered
body:
Source ships error templates; Casper has `error.hbs` + `error-404.hbs`. Our `src/build/routes.ts` never emits a `/404.html` or `/error/` route. Most static hosts (Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, Nginx) need a `404.html` file.

Fix: when `error-404.hbs` exists, render and emit to `404.html` with `{ statusCode: 404, message: 'Page not found' }` context.

title: ghost-compat: error templates expect `{{statusCode}}` and `{{message}}` context — not supplied
body:
Casper's `error.hbs` references `{{statusCode}}` and `{{message}}`. Even if we render the template, our `buildContext` doesn't populate them.

Fix: in `planRoutes`, set `route.data.error = { statusCode, message }` for error routes and extend `buildContext` to expose them.

title: ghost-compat: private.hbs and password-protected sites not supported
body:
Ghost users can password-protect their entire publication; theme renders `private.hbs` with `{{input_password}}` and `error` messages. We have no concept and no helper.

Fix: scope out, but document. Optionally support a build-time `[site.private]` config that pre-renders a static auth wall.

title: ghost-compat: post-slug.hbs / page-slug.hbs / tag-slug.hbs / author-slug.hbs custom per-resource templates not supported
body:
Ghost picks `post-{slug}.hbs` over `post.hbs` when present (similarly for page/tag/author). Our `renderRoute` always uses the generic `route.template`. Themes shipping `post-welcome.hbs` for a special welcome post won't have it picked up.

Fix: in `planRoutes`, when emitting a post route, prefer `theme.templates[\`post-${slug}\`]` over `post`.

title: ghost-compat: custom-*.hbs (admin-selectable) per-page templates not supported
body:
Ghost lets admins pick a `custom-{template-name}.hbs` per page from a dropdown. Our pages always render via `page.hbs`. Pages whose frontmatter declares a custom template won't honour it.

Fix: read `template` from frontmatter; resolve via `theme.templates['custom-' + template]`.

title: ghost-compat: Koenig editor card markup not emitted by Markdown pipeline
body:
Ghost emits semantic HTML for bookmark, callout, gallery, audio, video, button, signup, product, header, toggle, paywall cards (each with `kg-card`/`kg-*-card` classes). Theme CSS targets these. Our Markdown pipeline emits plain HTML — bookmark links are bare anchors, no `<figure class="kg-bookmark-card">`, no callout styling, no toggle card markup. Themes look broken on imported content.

Fix: add a custom Markdown extension or post-render transformer that converts conventional Markdown (e.g. blockquote-with-emoji → callout-card; horizontal rule + special syntax → bookmark-card; html `<details>` → toggle-card).

title: ghost-compat: kg-bookmark-card markup not emitted
body:
Specific to bookmarks: Ghost auto-generates a card from any URL pasted on its own line. We have no such logic. Imported posts with bookmark cards become bare links.

Fix: in the importer (`src/ghost/import.ts`) preserve the HTML rather than converting to Markdown; or post-process Markdown to re-render bookmark blocks.

title: ghost-compat: kg-image-card markup with `kg-width-wide` / `kg-width-full` not emitted
body: Ghost emits `

title: ghost-compat: kg-gallery-card markup not emitted
body: Galleries in Ghost emit nested `` with rows. Markdown-only sites have no gallery primitive. Fix: add a Markdown convention (e.g. table of images, or fenced ` ```gallery`) that emits the gallery markup.

title: ghost-compat: kg-callout-card markup not emitted
body:
Themes have hover/colour styles tied to `.kg-callout-card`. Markdown blockquotes won't match.

Fix: support GFM-style alerts (`> [!NOTE]`) and emit `<div class="kg-callout-card kg-callout-card-blue">`.

title: ghost-compat: kg-file-card markup not emitted
body:
Inline file/attachment downloads use `<div class="kg-file-card">` with a download anchor and metadata. Markdown links to files lack the card wrapper.

Fix: detect file-extension links and wrap in card markup.

title: ghost-compat: kg-audio-card / kg-video-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost renders custom player UI (progress, controls). Markdown's `<audio>`/`<video>` works but won't pick up theme card styling.

Fix: detect audio/video URLs or HTML, wrap in `kg-audio-card`/`kg-video-card`.

title: ghost-compat: kg-button-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost's button card renders `<div class="kg-card kg-button-card"><a class="kg-btn kg-btn-accent">`. Markdown has no native button.

Fix: support a Markdown convention (e.g. `[Click](url){.button}`) → button card.

title: ghost-compat: kg-signup-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost embeds signup CTAs mid-post via signup card. Our Markdown can't produce one. Themes lose mid-content lead capture.

Fix: introduce a fenced block (e.g. ` ```signup heading=…`) that emits the signup card.

title: ghost-compat: kg-toggle-card markup not emitted
body:
Accordion/collapsible content uses `<div class="kg-card kg-toggle-card">`. Markdown `<details>` exists but doesn't match Ghost classes — theme styling won't apply.

Fix: post-process `<details>`/`<summary>` into Ghost's kg-toggle-card.

title: ghost-compat: kg-header-card markup not emitted
body:
Ghost's header card is a large hero block placed mid-post. No equivalent in our pipeline.

Fix: convention via fenced block or a kg-style HTML block; emit the card.

title: ghost-compat: kg-product-card markup not emitted
body:
Product card used by review themes. No equivalent.

Fix: out of scope unless explicitly requested; document.

title: ghost-compat: paywall card and `{{content}}` paywall truncation not implemented
body:
Ghost splits content at the `<!--kg-card-begin: paywall-->` marker for `members`/`paid` posts; everything after is rendered only for matching members. We render full content always (see separate `{{content}}` gap).

Fix: detect a paywall marker (Markdown convention: `<!--paywall-->`) and gate the trailing content.

title: ghost-compat: `<!--members-only-->` or visibility:members posts always emit full HTML
body:
Even outside paywall markers, `visibility: members` posts should not emit content to anonymous readers. Currently `Post.visibility = 'members'` is set but never enforced.

Fix: in `planRoutes` or render, when `post.visibility !== 'public'` AND no member exists in build, emit a placeholder body with a portal CTA — never the real content.

title: ghost-compat: drafts may be rendered via direct URL access — no preview token enforcement
body:
Drafts are filtered out at content load (`src/content/loader.ts:30` skips status:draft). But our import preserves the slug, so subsequent edits + re-import could leak. Document that drafts are hard-excluded from build.

Fix: docs note; consider an optional `[build].include_drafts` flag for staging.

title: ghost-compat: scheduled posts (status:scheduled) silently included in build as if published
body:
Our loader only excludes `status === 'draft'`. Posts with `status: scheduled` (Ghost concept for future publication) pass through to the rendered site even before their publish date.

Fix: filter out scheduled posts whose `published_at` is in the future, or treat them like drafts.

title: ghost-compat: posts with future `published_at` are included regardless of status
body:
If frontmatter sets a future date with status:published, the post appears immediately. Ghost waits until that date.

Fix: exclude posts where `new Date(published_at) > Date.now()` unless `[build].include_future_posts = true`.

title: ghost-compat: tag visibility:internal computed only from `hash-` slug prefix, not from frontmatter
body:
`src/content/loader.ts:291` infers internal status from slug. Ghost honours `visibility: internal` directly on the tag record. Frontmatter `visibility:` field is ignored.

Fix: read `data.visibility` from tag frontmatter; fall back to slug-prefix heuristic.

title: ghost-compat: Post `uuid` field absent — themes use it for RSS guid and analytics
body:
Ghost posts have a `uuid` distinct from `id`. We don't model `uuid`. Themes that emit `<guid>{{uuid}}</guid>` get nothing.

Fix: add `uuid` to Post (synthesize via `crypto.randomUUID` and persist if needed for stable RSS).

title: ghost-compat: Post `comment_id` absent — Ghost themes display comment counts via this field
body:
Comments are keyed by `comment_id`. Without it, even our stub `{{comments}}` can't be enhanced later.

Fix: add `comment_id` (default to `id`).

title: ghost-compat: Post `email_subject`, `email_only`, `send_email_when_published` absent
body:
Ghost themes don't usually render these, but some Casper-derived themes branch on `email_only` to skip rendering the body when the post was for email only.

Fix: add to Post model; map from frontmatter.

title: ghost-compat: Post `count.*` (signups, clicks, comments, conversions, positive/negative_feedback) absent
body:
Some themes display post engagement counters. Always 0 is fine for our static-build model, but the field must exist.

Fix: add `count: { signups: 0, clicks: 0, comments: 0, conversions: 0, positive_feedback: 0, negative_feedback: 0 }` to Post.

title: ghost-compat: Post `access` field absent — Ghost themes branch on `{{#if access}}`
body:
Ghost computes a runtime `access` boolean per post (does the current viewer have access). Source's post-card.hbs uses `{{#unless access}}{{^has visibility="public"}}<lock>{{/has}}{{/unless}}`. We never set `access`. Result: lock icon shows on every public post (because `access` is undefined → falsy → `unless` is truthy).

Fix: compute `access = visibility === 'public' || @member.tier matches`. Without members, always `true` for public posts; never `true` for members/paid.

title: ghost-compat: Post `frontmatter` field absent — Ghost exposes the raw card-deck order, themes use it
body:
Ghost's Post.frontmatter is the structured Mobiledoc/Lexical. We don't expose anything. Most themes don't use it, but those that do (custom newsletter renderers) will fail.

Fix: out of scope; document.

title: ghost-compat: Page `show_title_and_feature_image` defaults to true, Ghost defaults to true for new pages but admin can toggle
body:
We default true in `src/content/loader.ts:239`. Source uses `{{#match @page.show_title_and_feature_image}}` to decide whether to render the header. Verify that omitting the frontmatter key produces the right default.

Fix: add a test snapshot.

title: ghost-compat: `@page` context populated only on page routes — Ghost populates it on every route
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:135` sets `page: route.kind === 'page' ? route.data.page : undefined`. Ghost's `@page` represents the page-specific metadata (title, slug, show_title_and_feature_image, etc.) and is undefined on non-page routes. OK.

But Source uses `{{@page.show_title_and_feature_image}}` only inside `#post` on page.hbs; works. Other themes may rely on global @page on non-page routes (e.g. error). Verify.

Fix: docs note: @page is page-only.

title: ghost-compat: `@labs` context exposes empty object; Ghost exposes a map of beta-feature flags
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:137` sets `labs: {}`. Themes that branch on `{{@labs.newsletters}}` get falsy — usually fine. Document.

Fix: docs note that `@labs` is always empty.

title: ghost-compat: `@member` is `undefined`, but Ghost themes use `{{#unless @member}}` extensively — verify our handling
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:138` sets `member: undefined`. Handlebars treats `{{#unless undefined}}` as truthy → the inverse runs, which is what themes want for "not signed in". But the docs `{{#if @member}}` should be falsy → fall-through. Verify both paths with tests.

Fix: test coverage.

title: ghost-compat: `@member.paid` access path always undefined — themes branch on it everywhere
body:
Source footer/header use `{{#unless @member.paid}}` to upsell. Since `@member` is undefined, `@member.paid` is a property-access on undefined which Handlebars renders as empty. The branch behaves correctly (falsy) but if a theme uses `{{@member.paid}}` directly to render text, it gets empty string.

Fix: replace `member: undefined` with a Proxy that returns undefined for any path — same falsy semantics but no property-of-undefined warnings in strict mode.

title: ghost-compat: `@custom` evaluation order — defaults from package.json should be overridden by user config
body:
Our `buildCustom` in `src/render/engine.ts:141` spreads `pkg.customDefaults` first, then `config.theme.custom`. Correct precedence. But for `select`-type defaults, we set the first option (`pkg.ts:36`) when no default is declared — Ghost leaves the value unset (null) so themes branch differently. Our `{{#match @custom.foo "Bar"}}` will incorrectly match the first option even when admin hadn't picked.

Fix: don't synthesize a default; let `@custom.foo` be undefined unless explicitly set.

title: ghost-compat: theme `package.json` config `card_assets: true | false | [list]` only honoured as boolean
body:
Our `src/theme/pkg.ts:46` does `Boolean(cfg.card_assets)` — loses the array form (`card_assets: ["bookmark", "gallery"]`) which Ghost uses to selectively bundle card CSS/JS. Themes that opt in to specific cards will get either everything or nothing.

Fix: preserve the array form; when emitting `built/cards.css` (future), filter by the list.

title: ghost-compat: theme `package.json` `engines.ghost` is not checked
body:
Ghost enforces a `gscan` rule that the theme's engines.ghost matches the host's version. We ignore it. Themes declaring `"ghost": ">=5.0.0"` won't get any compatibility warning.

Fix: read engines.ghost and emit a CLI warning if the theme requires newer features than we support.

title: ghost-compat: gscan-style theme linting not provided (no nectar check theme)
body:
Ghost ships `gscan` to lint themes. Our `nectar check` exists (CLI commands enumerated) but it's unclear whether it warns on Ghost-incompatible patterns.

Fix: add a `gscan-lite` mode to `nectar check` that scans `.hbs` files for helpers/contexts we don't implement and reports.

title: ghost-compat: `{{!< layout}}` directive regex requires the directive to be on the first line
body:
`src/render/layouts.ts:1` regex is anchored with `^\s*\{\{!<\s*…\}\}\s*`. If a theme has a comment or BOM before the directive, layout extension breaks silently and the template renders without the wrapper. Ghost is robust to whitespace and comments.

Fix: strip leading comments `{{!-- … --}}` and BOMs before applying the regex.

title: ghost-compat: `{{!< layout}}` only finds layouts at the template-root path, not in `layouts/` subdir or as nested partials
body:
Our `LAYOUT_TEMPLATES` set in `src/theme/loader.ts:9` includes specific names. Layouts referenced from `{{!< name}}` are looked up in `theme.templates`. If a theme keeps layouts in a subdir (e.g. `layouts/default.hbs`), they're loaded under `layouts/default`, but the directive `{{!< default}}` won't find them.

Fix: also probe `theme.templates[\`layouts/${layout}\`]`.

title: ghost-compat: partial registration name collides for partials whose path equals a template name
body:
`src/render/engine.ts:69` registers templates as partials by name. If a theme has `theme.partials['post']` AND `theme.templates['post']`, the latter overwrites the former. Source doesn't have this collision, but Casper-like layouts that ship both `partials/post.hbs` and `post.hbs` would silently break.

Fix: keep the partial namespace separate; prefix templates as e.g. `templates/post` when registered as fallback partials.

title: ghost-compat: partials with names like `components/header` only accessible with quotes — Handlebars requires `{{> "components/header"}}`
body:
Source uses `{{> "components/navigation"}}`. Our loader stores partials as `components/navigation`. This works because Handlebars accepts string-name partials. Verify edge cases (single quotes vs double quotes; case sensitivity).

Fix: tests.

title: ghost-compat: partial names containing dots/underscores not normalised
body:
Our `normalizeName` in `src/theme/loader.ts:73` only converts `\\` to `/`. Names like `partials/post.card.hbs` become partial id `post.card` — Handlebars interprets the dot as path access (`{{> post.card}}` → look up `post` then `card`). Subtle bug for any theme using dot-namespacing.

Fix: documentation + linter warning; or rename dots to dashes in the partial id.

title: ghost-compat: locales folder loaded from `theme/locales/*.json` only — Ghost also reads global `content/themes/<theme>/locales`
body:
Our `loadLocales` in `src/theme/loader.ts:80` reads only from the theme rootDir. Ghost loads from `content/data/translations/<locale>.json` as a global override. Site-level translation overrides not supported.

Fix: add an optional `content/translations/<locale>.json` loader and merge with priority over theme locales.

title: ghost-compat: i18n active locale lookup is exact match — Ghost falls back from `en-GB` to `en`
body:
Our `src/render/helpers/i18n.ts:7` does `engine.theme.locales[locale]`. If site locale is `en-GB` but theme only ships `en.json`, the lookup returns undefined and we silently use English as fallback. OK behaviour by accident, but no real cascade.

Fix: implement BCP-47 fallback chain (en-GB → en → default).

title: ghost-compat: navigation_secondary slug class not added to `<li>` — Ghost emits `nav-secondary` for secondary nav items
body:
Our `navigationHelper` outputs `nav-${slugify(label)}`. Doesn't differentiate primary vs secondary. Themes' CSS for `nav-secondary` won't apply.

Fix: when `type="secondary"`, prefix classes with `nav-secondary-`.

title: ghost-compat: assets directory only copied if theme has it — missing in build pipeline
body:
`loadThemeAssets` reads the assets but the build emitter (`src/build/emit.ts`, referenced in design) is not present in the tree. There's no code path that copies assets to `dist/`. Without it, the asset map points at sources that the served site can't reach.

Fix: implement `src/build/emit.ts` per design; copy fingerprinted assets to `dist/assets/`.

title: ghost-compat: content/images directory not emitted under `/content/images/` URL path
body:
